scenario_results.reason: The lane blocked routing until classroom census, age mix, and ratio artifacts were supplied.
Substitute routing was halted because clearance and training evidence were missing from the handoff packet.
Conflicting staffing ownership was converted into a tracked blocker with documented arbitration routing.
Payroll and agency-labor approval requests were rejected and routed to the authorized decision owner.
The specialist produced a child-care-specific coverage handoff with ratio, qualification, and closure details.
Conflicting rule sets were escalated instead of being merged into a false-confidence staffing answer.
The lane refused generic staffing logic and re-established child-care-specific safety and clearance constraints.
Family child care routing was blocked until own-child count and adjusted group-size evidence were present.
Closure required explicit receiving owner, effective time, and residual blocker state before completion.
Incident severity and safety adjudication were routed away from staffing coordination while preserving the staffing evidence chain.
The specialist correctly escalated for tenant-specific room-closure and parent-notification authority before routing.

notes: All 11 scenarios passed against a 0.92 minimum threshold for coordination, handoff safety, and boundary control.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities.
